title: Extend golden set with paraphrase + distractor queries; add hybrid floor regression guard
Codex PreToolUse beacon matcher 'Bash' may never fire — verify Codex shell tool name (E2E)
description:
integrations/codex/hooks/hooks.json wires the PreToolUse beacon with matcher "Bash" (cloned from the Claude Code hook shape, which Codex mirrors per the 2026-06 hook-parity research). But Codex CLI's shell tool is historically named 'shell'/'local_shell', NOT 'Bash'. If the matcher value must equal Codex's tool name, the beacon ('qmemd hook beacon') NEVER fires on Codex and fails silently (no error, just absent context).

Research fact (qmemd-integration-hook-parity-researched-2026-06-25-cursor) says 'PreToolUse reliable for Bash/shell' but is ambiguous on the matcher VALUE the manifest must use.

Action: E2E-install the Codex plugin against a real Codex CLI (hooks engine on, codex_hooks=true), trigger a shell tool call, confirm the beacon fires. If it doesn't, change the matcher to Codex's actual shell tool name (likely 'shell' / 'local_shell' / a regex covering both). Gate this BEFORE 'codex plugin marketplace add Ciel2142/qmemd'.

File: integrations/codex/hooks/hooks.json. Cursor side is fine (no matcher -> fires on all tools; beacon is throttled+fail-open). Found in review of commit 1bc2002.

title: Fix npm CI OIDC Trusted Publishing — release.yml E404s on fresh-version publish
description:
release.yml (publish on GitHub Release published, 'npm publish --access public --provenance', id-token:write) FAILS for every fresh version: v0.5.1 CI run 28169432529 + its --failed re-run both errored 'E404 PUT https://registry.npmjs.org/@ciel2142/qmemd — not found or no permission'. 0.5.1 was absent from the registry, so the 404 is auth-DENIED, not existence-masking. OIDC trusted publishing has NEVER actually published a fresh version — every live version (0.1.0..0.5.0) shipped via MANUAL local 'npm publish' as owner ciel2142. The earlier 'RESOLVED' note was a false positive: a re-run's E403 'cannot publish over 0.5.0' only appeared because 0.5.0 had been published manually first (E403 proves the version exists, not that OIDC auth works).

Root cause (likely): the trusted-publisher binding on npmjs.com is absent or mismatched. npm CLI can neither read nor set it.

Fix (USER, web UI): npmjs.com -> @ciel2142/qmemd -> Settings -> Publishing access -> add GitHub Actions trusted publisher: org/repo Ciel2142/qmemd, workflow file 'release.yml', environment BLANK (release.yml declares no environment:). Confirm the exact workflow filename + that the repo owner matches the OIDC 'sub'. Then cut a throwaway patch release (or re-run a release job for an UNPUBLISHED version) and confirm the publish step goes green WITHOUT a prior manual publish of that version.

Acceptance: a fresh vX.Y.Z publishes to npm purely from CI (with provenance), no manual fallback. Until then, releases use the manual path (npm publish --access public as ciel2142, no provenance). Found during 0.5.1 release.

title: Decide hybrid-by-default vs lex-first recall policy (measure warm cost/benefit)
description:
Measured (golden set, 2026-06-24): lex P@1=0.867 @ ~0ms; hybrid P@1=0.933 @ ~580ms warm (cold 1660ms = model-load artifact). The +0.067 P@1 uplift is arithmetically all from the 3 multi-relevant queries (the 12 single-relevant anchors already get lex P@1=1.0). The golden set is lex-trivial so it understates hybrid's real (semantic) value.

Question: is hybrid-by-default worth +580ms/query on small per-project corpora (lex is ~0ms)?

Tasks:
(a) measure warm lex-vs-hybrid on a REAL corpus (needs the paraphrase/distractor set from R3);
(b) evaluate a lex-first cascade — run lex, escalate to hybrid only when lex returns < N hits or low BM25 confidence;
(c) decide: cascade, keep hybrid default (documented), or corpus-size-aware default.

Acceptance: a measured recommendation + the chosen default wired into recallQuery (engine.ts:1770+). The warm daemon (qmemd-vuk) already hides cold-load, so this is purely the 580ms-vs-0ms steady-state tradeoff.

title: Index platform/project fields to drop recall over-fetch + corpus-wide refetch
description:
platform and project are not index fields, so recallQuery over-fetches fetchLimit = limit * MEMORY_TYPES.length (=4) whenever a type/platform/project gate is active (engine.ts:1781), parses each candidate file to gate (engine.ts:1899-1910), and on a gated underflow refetches the WHOLE corpus and reranks it (engine.ts:1959-1968, qmemd-amm). Indexing platform/project removes the refetch and the O(N) per-file reads.

Note: latency win is modest — qmd caps rerank candidates at topK=40, so cutting fetchLimit helps only past that. The real payoff is correctness/simplicity and scaling as the corpus grows (tens -> hundreds of facts).

Acceptance: gate predicates served from the index; the corpus-wide refetch path removed; recall results unchanged on the golden set.

title: Capability-gated default recall mode
description: Default qmemd recall to lex on hosts with no working GPU backend; keep hybrid on Mac/Metal, working CUDA/Vulkan, and whenever a warm daemon is reachable. New src/capability.ts owns explicit-mode resolver + cached no-model GPU probe + composition helpers; CLI cold path and MCP stdio compute lexOnly from it; HTTP daemon stays hybrid. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-26-capability-gated-recall-mode.md

title: T1: resolveExplicitMode — flags + env precedence (no probe)
description: Create src/capability.ts + test/capability.test.ts. Types GpuVerdict/RecallMode/ModePolicy/ExplicitSource/ExplicitDecision + resolveExplicitMode(flags,env). Precedence: lex-flag > hybrid-flag > QMEMD_RECALL_MODE > QMD_FORCE_CPU > QMD_LLAMA_GPU > auto. Plan Task 1.

title: T2: GPU-backend probe + isCapableBackend + nlc version
description: Append to capability.ts: isCapableBackend, probeGpuBackend (getLlama build:never, no model, never throws), nodeLlamaCppVersion. Unit-test only pure pieces — never call real probe. Plan Task 2.

title: T3: cached getGpuCapability with version invalidation
description: Append CapabilityCache/CapabilityDeps + getGpuCapability: probe once, cache <cacheDir>/gpu-capability.json, re-probe on missing/corrupt/version-change. Injectable deps; no real probe in tests. Plan Task 3.

title: T4: autoRecallMode + shouldAutoResolve composition
description: Append autoRecallMode (capable=>hybrid else lex) + shouldAutoResolve(lexOnly,warmServer). Plan Task 4.

title: T5: CLI — --hybrid flag, mode resolution, downgrade note
description: Modify src/cli/qmemd.ts + test/cli.test.ts: add --hybrid; --lex/--hybrid mutually exclusive; daemon gate mode!=lex; compute lexOnly via resolveExplicitMode+autoRecallMode; stderr downgrade note (force-cpu/auto only, not user-chosen lex); usage strings. Plan Task 5.

title: T6: MCP — warm-daemon exemption + stdio auto-resolution
description: Modify src/mcp/server.ts + test/mcp.test.ts: add warmServer + resolveAutoMode to MemoryServerOptions; apply shouldAutoResolve in stdio recall; warmServer:true on HTTP daemon. Plan Task 6.

title: T7: build + full suite + manual smoke
description: npm run build (tsc strict 0 errors); npm test all green no model load; manual smoke: --lex --hybrid => exit1 mutual-exclusion; QMD_FORCE_CPU=1 recall => stderr lex note + results. Plan Task 7.

title: Configure npm Trusted Publisher for @ciel2142/qmemd before first release-triggered publish
description: release.yml publishes via OIDC Trusted Publishing (no NPM_TOKEN). Until the trusted publisher is registered on npmjs.com, the publish job fails auth. One-time manual step (user, web UI): npmjs.com -> @ciel2142/qmemd -> Settings -> Trusted Publishing -> add GitHub Actions publisher: org/repo Ciel2142/qmemd, workflow file 'release.yml' (environment optional). After that, publishing = create a GitHub Release with tag vX.Y.Z (package.json must already be bumped to match; the workflow guards it). Requires npm CLI >=11.5 on the runner (release.yml does 'npm install -g npm@latest'). Ref: docs.npmjs.com trusted publishers / GitHub OIDC.

title: Recalibrate DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE against a real calibration curve (not anecdote)
description:
DEFAULT_MIN_SCORE=0.575 (engine.ts:1621) was set by anecdote — lowered from 0.6 to keep one real fact observed at 0.581. Measured on the synthetic golden set: reranker is bimodal (noise ~0.500 tail 0.578, relevant ~0.731); clean window (0.578, 0.690], midpoint ~0.63; 0.575 admits 2/290 noise while any floor in [0.600,0.675] keeps the same 15/18 relevant with 0 noise. BUT 0.63 would drop the documented 0.581 fact, and rerankScore is non-deterministic — so do NOT bump the number blind.

Tasks: derive the floor from R3's real-corpus curve; pick a value maximizing margin from both clusters given run-to-run jitter; document the basis in the code comment (replace the n=1 anecdote).

Acceptance: floor value backed by a measured curve, not a single observation. Blocked by the R3 harness.

title: SessionStart hooks emit raw stdout / no suppressOutput → visible 'hook success:' banner
description:
Plugin SessionStart hooks inject context but trigger the visible 'hook success:' banner that crowds the user's first prompt (qmemd memory: claude-code-sessionstart-hook-success-banners-visible). Two emitters:
1. hooks/inject-rule.mjs buildSessionContext() emits {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:SessionStart,additionalContext}} — MISSING suppressOutput:true.
2. CLI 'qmemd recall --session' (src/cli/qmemd.ts ~293) does console.log(out) RAW — no JSON envelope at all; run-qmemd.mjs passes it straight through.
Fix: wrap both as {suppressOutput:true,hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:SessionStart,additionalContext:<text>}}. Pre-existing on main (old session-start.sh was also raw); PR #1 made the rule net-better but did not add suppressOutput. recallSession() engine string contract unchanged (only CLI stdout wrapping). TDD: cli.test.ts + plugin.test.ts.
close_reason: Wrapped both SessionStart emitters in {suppressOutput:true, hookSpecificOutput.{hookEventName:SessionStart, additionalContext}}: hooks/inject-rule.mjs buildSessionContext() + CLI 'recall --session' (src/cli/qmemd.ts). recallSession() engine string unchanged. TDD: +1 plugin.test.ts (suppressOutput), +2 cli.test.ts (envelope + empty-snapshot). Full suite 858/0. Verified on built dist: both emit suppressOutput=true SessionStart envelope.

title: Measure session-snapshot hit-rate / injection-precision before tuning projectLimit/budget
description:
recallSession (engine.ts:511) selects facts by recency only (+ manual pins); projectLimit=5 and budget=2000 bytes are arbitrary defaults with no measurement (engine.ts:512-513). With 53 project facts for this repo only the 5 newest surface at session start — automatic relevance plays no role until an explicit recall. Snapshot hit-rate / injection-precision and net-context ROI (memory ON vs OFF) are unbuilt axes.

Tasks: build a snapshot injection-precision metric (did injected facts match what the session actually needed?); only then tune projectLimit/budget or add a relevance signal to the snapshot.

Acceptance: a measured snapshot hit-rate baseline + a data-backed projectLimit/budget recommendation.
